result:
The full BMad pipeline is complete — planning through implementation. Here's the summary.

## Compound Strength App — end-to-end run complete

I ran the entire workflow autonomously on Fast path, pausing once for the contested program-canon decisions (you dismissed, so I proceeded with pinned, reversible defaults).

### Planning phase (gates 1–3) — all green
| Artifact | Location | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Product brief | `planning-artifacts/product-brief.md` | saved as durable artifact |
| **PRD** | `prds/.../prd.md` + `addendum.md` | 34 FRs, 8 features; reviewer gate passed (FR-ID hygiene + RPE/Madcow fixes applied) |
| **UX** | `ux-designs/.../DESIGN.md` + `EXPERIENCE.md` | dark/glanceable/one-handed; 2 HTML mocks |
| **Architecture** | `architecture.md` | 4-layer pure-domain design, full FR→module traceability |
| **Epics/stories** | `epics.md` | 7 epics, 34 stories, Given/When/Then ACs |
| **Readiness** | `readiness-report.md` | independent audit: READY, 34/34 FRs; 1 HIGH + 4 MEDIUM fixed in-place |
| **Sprint plan** | `implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml` | 34 stories tracked |

### Implementation (gate 4) — core verified, full app as source
- **`npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm test` → 75 passing / 18 suites** (pure Node).
- **Epic 1 (domain engine) + Epic 2 (persistence): implemented and test-verified.** All 7 programs with canon-correct prescribe/advance, plate calculator, e1RM, PR detection, warm-up, rotation, recommendation, timestamp rest-timer math, seed-all-programs; SQLite schema + migrator + repositories tested against **real SQLite** (Node 26 `node:sqlite`), including the FR-33 program-switch-preserves-history guarantee.
- **Epics 3–7 (services, stores, hooks, components, onboarding, tabs, completion/PR, Live Activity scaffold): implemented as reviewable source** — 36 UI/app/service/plugin files. Their runtime/native verification is the platform team's follow-up, per the sprint's explicit no-build boundary.

### Key engineering decisions
- The Expo SDK 56 stack **isn't resolvable** in this environment (and building is forbidden this sprint), so I verify the pure layers with a dev-toolchain-only `package.json` and keep the full SDK-56 manifest as `package.app.json` for the platform team. The domain/data layers import nothing from Expo by design — that's what makes them testable now.
- Pinned defaults (reversible, one file each): nSuns **4-day LP**, Reddit PPL **3-strike** deload, 5×3 **app-defined**.

### Handoff
`implementation-artifacts/HANDOFF.md` documents exactly what's verified vs. deferred, how to verify (`npm install && npm test && npm run typecheck`), and the platform team's next steps (install SDK 56, `tsconfig.app.json` typecheck, prebuild/run, wire device-only behaviors, add `jest-expo` component tests).

This matches the brief's bar: prioritize the core, deliver a complete reviewable PR, with the domain logic (the headline success criterion) proven by tests rather than asserted.

questions.question: Which nSuns 5/3/1 LP variant should be the canonical implementation? (Brief flagged nSuns as contested.)

options.label: 4-day LP (Recommended)
options.description: The wiki/LiftVault documented baseline and lowest-risk default. My current pin. Day-count model makes adding 5/6-day later cheap.

options.label: 5-day LP
options.description: The most *popular* variant in practice (more frequency). Slightly more setup but what most users picture as 'nSuns'.

options.label: 6-day LP
options.description: Highest volume/frequency. Best fit for advanced users training 6 days, overlaps with the PPL audience.

questions.question: Reddit PPL (Metallicadpa) deload trigger — your brief said 'double-failure' but the current published canon is 3 consecutive failures. Which wins?

options.label: 3 consecutive failures (Recommended)
options.description: Matches the current Metallicadpa v3.0 / r/Fitness wiki canon. My current pin. Deviates from the brief's wording.

options.label: 2 consecutive failures
options.description: Honors the brief's literal 'double-failure' wording. Faster to deload; matches older PPL forks.

questions.question: The remaining 5 open items (5×3 app-defined mechanics, 5/3/1 includes deload week, Madcow 12.5% interval, round-down for non-loadable weights, beginner→linear recommendation map) — I've pinned standard defaults for all. Handle how?

options.label: Keep all my defaults (Recommended)
options.description: Proceed to UX/architecture with the pinned defaults; all are documented in §8 + Assumptions Index and reversible later.

options.label: Let's review them
options.description: Pause and walk through each of the 5 before continuing. Adds a round-trip but locks every canon choice now.
